title: Rich Velvety Kenyan Pumpkin Mousse
description: A sophisticated date-night dessert combining smooth Kenyan pumpkin puree with a unique sansho pepper kick and namul-style blanched greens.
instructions: Steam the pumpkin cubes until they are fork-tender and allow them to cool completely.
Blanch the baby spinach in boiling water for thirty seconds then plunge it into an ice bath.
Squeeze all excess moisture from the spinach and toss it with sesame oil and a pinch of salt.
Place the cooled pumpkin into a high-speed blender and process until perfectly smooth.
Whip the chilled coconut cream in a separate bowl until it forms stiff peaks.
Fold the pumpkin puree into the whipped coconut cream gently using a rubber spatula.
Stir in the maple syrup, vanilla extract, and ground sansho pepper until well combined.
Divide the mousse into two elegant glass bowls and refrigerate for at least two hours.
Top each serving with a small nest of the seasoned namul spinach just before serving.
Dust the final dish with an extra pinch of sansho pepper for aromatic intensity.
